title: How to create a new protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2gpf9","text":"This protocol describes how to create a new protocol on protocols.io.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"algpc","text":"protocols.io allows you to add as much detail as needed to your protocol. On the right-hand side of the editor, you can find components what allow you detailed information. Using components such as for example duration will help you and others save time while running the experiment as there will be a set timer available in the run. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dtvi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mexs4","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":{}},{"key":"fs8xu","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"5tkdj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ufgr","text":"Components include: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5u8sb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9o9f","text":"Amount: use if there is a specific amount of a reagent needed. The \"scale\" functionality in an experiment run will allow you to easily scale the protocol if this comonent is used. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11kad","text":"Duration: add timers to your protocol. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2e1q2","text":"Concentration: adding exact concentration details. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r2ri","text":"Temerature: select between a specific temperature or room temperature. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14ini","text":"Equipment: search for existing equipent or create a new one adding details such as brand, SKU, and specifications of the equipment. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fksf4","text":"Reagent: search for available reagent or add a new one. The reagents will have catalog numbers that can link directly to the vendors site where the exact reagent can be purchased. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5maqc","text":"Command: for computational workflows, add your command lines. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfvgk","text":"Dataset: make your dataset available and link to it with this component. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"elrrs","text":"Software: add the software you used for your methods. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ve48","text":"Note: if you need to additional notes for a step, you can add them with this component. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rioq","text":"Safety Information: if a step includes hazardous materials, mark them clearly with the saftety information component. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"t42o","text":"Expected Result: let the reader know which result is expected after certain steps. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88dun","text":"Goto: if steps are repeated throughout the process you can link to previous steps with this component. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6k6uj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qb53","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"db95m","text":"In addition to the components you can work with to make your protocol as detailed as possible, you can find some additional features on the top that let you add links, images, videos, files, formulas, and tables to your protocol for any step. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjkho","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"expfm","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1vn3k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"61581D06341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":476,"is_image":true,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"6r044\",\"text\":\"components \",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"Screenshot 2019-04-26 at 12.36.11.png","placeholder":"","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bga9fw26.png","width":141.31249999999997}},"1":{"type":"image","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"61581DA1341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":66,"is_image":true,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"Screenshot 2019-04-26 at 15.01.38.png","placeholder":"","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bgbbfw26.png","width":698}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b3nj8","text":"All edits you make to the protocol will save automatically. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Click on the + icon in the upper-right corner to open the main menu, and select 'New protocol'.
Add a Title . This will be the name your protocol.
Navigate to the Guidelines & Warnings tab on the top of the protocol editor. Warnings : add any specific warning related to your protocol
You have 2 ways to add steps in the protocol: Entering protocol steps from an existing text document that includes a numbered or pulletpoint list of steps. Entering protocol steps from scratch.
Add an abstract . This should be short description of your protocol. It should put your protocol in context and let the reader know what your protocol is all about.
For better discoverability, add a thubnail image to your protocol. This could either be a picture of your experiment, your results, or your institution logo.
Add all authors of this protocol. Names should be separated with commas. Affiliation : if possible, please add the name of the author’s lab and/or institute as an affiliatio
Add keywords to your protocol that will help you to organize you protocols and make it more discoverable for others when published.
Optional: add an External Link (URL) if there is any link to your lab website, publication or any further information.
If the protocol is part of a published manuscript, you can add the Manuscript Citation .
You can make your original protocol file avaiable by attaching it to the protocol as reference.
Guidelines : this space is for any guidelines, references and other useful information to accompany the protocol.

Before Start: this section allows you to add any needed preparations or information the reader should be aware of before starting the experiment. In the view and run modes, this section will appear right before the first step of the protocol.
Safefty Warnings: Please add any hazard or saftey precautions here if your protocol inlcudes working with hazardous materials.

Sensetive Content Warning: If your protocol contains sensitive content that some people may find disturbing please make sure this checkbox is checked. This will give a waring to readers when opening the protocol.
You can either search for existing reagents or add a new one.
If you are creating a new reagent, you will be asked to add the following information to it.

When you're done entering all information about the new reagent, click the SAVE button. If you would like to improt multiple reagents in bulk, you can to so by clicking the UPLOAD REAGENTS button wich will ask you to upload a CSV text fill with the required information.
Any addtional information about required materials can be added to the Materials Text box.
Click on "paste from a text file".
Paste your steps into the window that opended up.
Select between "Each number is a step." or "Each line is a step.".
Click the PREVIEW button.
If the preview looks good, click the ADD THESE STEPS TO PROTOCOL button. Your steps will be added to the protocol.

You can now edit your protocol further by introducing sections and using the comonents on the right hand side. Additional information regarding the components can be found in the Guielines section of this protocol.
Add the first section name of your protocol.

Add the content of the firts step while using the components on the righthand side. You can also insert images, videos, formulas, etc. with the editor option on the top. Addtional information about the available components can be found in the Guielines section of this protocol.

You can now continue adding addtional steps to your protocol. You have the following options for the next step: New Step: This option will add a new step that will belong to the same section as the previous one. New Section: This option will add a blank step with a starting new section. New Sub-Step: This option will add a substep of the current step. (So for example if your current step is 22 and you add a substep, the next step will be 22.1)
creator: Lenny Teytelman
